_comment: p95 latency budgets in milliseconds, per named performance scenario. The perf harness (tests/perf/, run nightly, not in the PR gate) writes measured p95s to .bench/perf-latest.json with shape {"results_ms": {"<name>": {"p95": <number>}}}. check-perf-budget.sh compares each measured p95 against its budget here and fails when a budget is exceeded. Budgets start generous (CI-runner variance is real) and ratchet DOWN as hot paths are optimized -- mirror the file-size / pyright budget discipline: when a deliberate change moves a number, edit it here with a one-line reason in bump_history.
bump_history: initial budgets seeded from the phase plan; unverified until the perf harness has run on a stable runner.
